name: Roborock QV 35A Robot Vacuum and Mop
tag: Best Value All-In-One Robot Vacuum Mop

specs.Main Brush: All-rubber anti-tangle spiral brush (SGS-certified 0% tangle rate)
specs.Dock Functions: Auto dust empty, auto water refill, mop self-wash, cold air dry

whatWorks: The all-rubber spiral main brush is SGS-certified for 0% hair tangling — owners with dogs and cats consistently report zero maintenance cutting hair out of the brush, the most common complaint about robot vacuums with traditional brushes.
45 dB in standard mode is among the quietest at this price tier. Owners working from home and those with noise-sensitive pets note it routinely as a reason they leave it running during the day.

Full dock automation at a mid-range price point — auto-empty, mop wash, auto-dry, auto-refill — is a recurring reason owners cite for choosing this over cheaper alternatives that require manual dock maintenance.
180-minute battery covers larger homes in a single pass without the recharge-and-resume interruptions that frustrate owners of shorter-runtime robots.
worthKnowing: Dock uses cold-air drying rather than heated air — the Qrevo S Pro and Curv add hot-water mop washing. Owners with heavy kitchen grease note the difference when comparing cleaning effectiveness on greasy floors.
2.4GHz Wi-Fi only — a consistent friction point in Reddit threads for owners using mesh networks that default to 5GHz bands.
Mop arms are fixed and cannot extend past the robot body, leaving a 1–2cm strip along baseboards and table legs unmopped.

commonComplaints.title: Obstacle detection struggles on dark hardwood…
commonComplaints.body: Obstacle detection struggles on dark hardwood and black tile — owners in r/RobotVacuums and on Amazon.ca consistently report the structured-light sensor loses contrast on dark surfaces and leaves zones unclean or cuts passes short.

commonComplaints.title: Dock water leaks in early weeks…
commonComplaints.body: Dock water leaks in early weeks of use come up repeatedly in owner threads — the dirty-water tank has been known to overflow below the dock. Owners recommend placing a tray underneath for the first month.

commonComplaints.title: High-pile carpet cleaning is a recurring…
commonComplaints.body: High-pile carpet cleaning is a recurring limitation — the 8,000Pa suction and 10mm mop lift are insufficient for deep pile, and owners with thick area rugs report noticeably more residue than on hard floors.

verdict: Owners who land in the QV 35A's sweet spot — hard floors, medium-pile carpet, pets, preference for hands-off operation — repeatedly describe it as the best value at this tier. The full-automation dock, anti-tangle brush, and quiet motor come up more often than any complaint. The limitations are specific: dark floors, high-pile carpet, and a desire for hot-water mop cleaning are the three conditions owners consistently name as reasons to step up.

realBuyers.quote: We only need to refill water every few days, wipe sensors occasionally, and swap the dustbin bag once a month. It's more like a cactus I forget about that thrives on neglect.
Three dogs and I haven't touched scissors once — the anti-tangle brush is the real deal. They pick up so much dog hair the bags go weekly.
The mopping is better than expected for an active household with dogs and hard floors. It genuinely removes footprints and dried spills, not just dampens the floor.

sources.title: RedditRecs: Roborock QV 35A — All Reddit Reviews

metaDescription: Roborock QV 35A review for Canadian buyers: 8,000Pa suction, full auto-wash dock, anti-tangle rubber brush, 45dB quiet operation. Real owner feedback and Canadian price.
introText: The Roborock QV 35A is a 2025 mid-range robot vacuum that bundles full dock automation — auto-empty, mop wash, mop dry, and tank refill — with 8,000Pa HyperForce suction and a rubber anti-tangle brush rated 0% hair tangle. It sits below the Qrevo Curv and S Pro in the lineup but offers the same hands-off maintenance experience at a lower price, making it the entry point into Roborock's full-service ecosystem in Canada.
